seeding_instructions.important: REQUIRED STEP: Seed these meters with 48h baseline history BEFORE sending any test payload.
seeding_instructions.seed_group_A: python utils/seed_normal_history.py --meter SE2303001 SE2303062 SE2303064 --group group_A --hours 48 --before '2026-06-18 10:00:00'
seeding_instructions.why_necessary: Without seeded history: rolling_mean = current reading, rolling_std = 0, z_score = 0. Statistical layer cannot fire. Test payloads represent FUTURE readings of seeded meters.
seeding_instructions.physics_note: All test values are derived from the exact same physics as generate_dataset.py and seed_normal_history.py: E_wh = load_kw * 0.5 * 1000, I = (load_kw*1000)/(V*PF), with voltage droop V = 230 - I*0.05. At 10:00 weekday, base load = 0.70 kW -> E = 350 Wh, V = 229.83 V, I = 3.311 A.

group_A_tests.description: group_A: energy_consumption + voltage + current + power_factor. Real meters: SE2303001, SE2303062, SE2303064. OBIS: 1.0.1.29.0.255=energy(Wh), 1.0.12.27.0.255=voltage(V), 1.0.11.27.0.255=current(A), 1.0.13.27.0.255=PF.

normal.description: Normal reading of SE2303001 at 10:00 after 48h seeded history. All layers should pass: no rule violation, z_score < 3.0, IF scores within normal range.

zscore_spike.description: Energy spike to 650 Wh on SE2303062. Rolling baseline ≈ 350 Wh mean, 52 Wh std. z = (650-350)/52 = 5.77, well above threshold of 3.0. Current rises proportionally with the increased load (same physics). Rule layer passes (no voltage/PF violation).

zscore_drop.description: Sudden energy drop to 15 Wh on SE2303064. z = (15-350)/52 = -6.44, well below -3.0. Current drops proportionally (minimal load). Could indicate connection fault, disconnection, or tamper. Rule layer passes (positive energy, voltage/PF normal).

same_hour_deviation.description: Energy 525 Wh on SE2303001. Historical avg same hour ≈ 350 Wh. Deviation = (525-350)/350 = 50%, above the 40% threshold. z = (525-350)/52 = 3.37, also above z-score threshold. Both statistical triggers fire. Rule layer passes.

voltage_sag.description: Voltage sag to 165 V on SE2303062 (below rule threshold of 180 V). Same active power (350 Wh) at lower voltage requires higher current: I = 700W / (165V * 0.92) = 4.61 A. This correlated current rise is the physical signature of a sag event.

voltage_swell.description: Voltage swell to 278 V on SE2303064 (above rule threshold of 270 V). Same active power at higher voltage means lower current: I = 700W / (278V * 0.92) = 2.74 A. Typically caused by capacitor bank switching or load shedding.

negative_energy.description: Negative energy on SE2303001 (-350 Wh). Indicates CT reversal, meter register rollover, or physical tampering. Current remains positive (current still flows). Rule fires immediately on sign check regardless of history.

power_factor_invalid.description: Power factor 1.05 on SE2303062 (above pf_max = 1.0 in settings.py). Physically impossible: PF cannot exceed 1.0. Indicates a meter firmware error or hardware fault. Rule fires immediately on bounds check.

power_factor_collapse.description: PF collapse to 0.65 on SE2303064. pf_min in settings.py = 0.0, so rule layer does NOT fire (0.65 > 0.0). This is purely an Isolation Forest / statistical anomaly. Active energy is unchanged (350 Wh); apparent energy rises to 538.5 VAh (S = P/PF = 700/0.65). Current rises to 4.69 A due to increased reactive component. The unusual E/I/PF combination is what the IF model detects.

expected_layer_result.rule_based: PASS (pf_min=0.0, pf_max=1.0 in settings.py — 0.65 is within bounds)
expected_layer_result.z_score: PASS (energy unchanged, current z-score may trigger depending on current rolling std)
expected_layer_result.isolation_forest: LIKELY TRIGGERED (trained on pf_collapse anomaly type at 0.55-0.72 range)

tamper_bypass.description: Tamper bypass on SE2303001. Energy suppressed to 30% of expected: 105 Wh (bypass routes current around the meter CT). Current stays at full expected level and is slightly elevated (3.64 A vs 3.31 A normal) due to bypass inefficiency. Unusual E/I ratio is the IF signature: low energy with high current is physically inconsistent for a legitimate meter.

expected_layer_result.rule_based: PASS (energy is positive, no voltage/PF violation)
expected_layer_result.isolation_forest: LIKELY TRIGGERED (tamper_bypass in training data — low E + full I pattern)

sustained_zero.description: Sustained zero consumption on SE2303062. Energy = 0 Wh, Current = 0 A, Voltage remains (grid connected but no load or meter disconnected). The zero_consumption_window rule requires 3+ consecutive zero readings to fire. Send this payload 3 times in sequence to trigger the window check.

group_V_tests.description: group_V: voltage + current only (no energy). OBIS: 1.0.12.27.0.255=voltage(V), 1.0.11.27.0.255=current(A). Real meters: A0825001, A32600225. Rolling statistics computed on current as the primary series (energy absent).

normal.description: Normal reading of A0825001 after seeded history. Voltage and current within expected range.

current_spike.description: Current spike to 11.59 A on A32600225 (3.5× normal 3.31 A). Voltage sags due to droop: V = 230 - 11.59*0.05 = 229.42 V. z_current = (11.59-3.31)/0.5 >> 3.0. Consistent with a sudden large inductive load connection.

expected_layer_result.rule_based: PASS (voltage 229.4 V is within 180-270 V bounds)

voltage_sag.description: Voltage sag to 165 V on A0825001 (below 180 V threshold). Current rises correspondingly at the same power level.

group_E_tests.description: group_E: energy_consumption only. OBIS: 1.0.1.29.0.255=energy(Wh). Real meters: SE000021, SE000022. Rolling statistics and z-score computed solely on energy.

normal.description: Normal energy reading of SE000021 after seeded history.

energy_spike.description: Energy spike to 650 Wh on SE000022. z = (650-350)/52 = 5.77, well above 3.0 threshold.

negative_energy.description: Negative energy on SE000021 (-350 Wh). Rule fires immediately on sign check.

group_B_tests.description: group_B: energy_consumption + apparent_import_energy + voltage. OBIS: 1.0.1.29.0.255=energy(Wh), 1.0.9.29.0.255=apparent_energy(VAh), 1.0.12.27.0.255=voltage(V). Real meters: SE0000009, SE0000011.

normal.description: Normal reading of SE0000009 after seeded history.

energy_spike_with_voltage_sag.description: Combined anomaly on SE0000011: energy spike to 650 Wh (z ≈ 5.77) AND voltage sag to 165 V. Apparent energy rises proportionally with the spike (706.5 VAh). The voltage sag triggers rule layer independently. This payload tests two simultaneous anomaly signals.

group_C_tests.description: group_C: energy_consumption + current only. OBIS: 1.0.1.29.0.255=energy(Wh), 1.0.11.27.0.255=current(A). Real meters: SE1338167, SE2104744.

normal.description: Normal reading of SE1338167 after seeded history.

energy_and_current_spike.description: Correlated energy+current spike on SE2104744. Energy to 650 Wh, current to 6.15 A (both rise proportionally as they should for a real load increase). z-score on energy = 5.77. The correlated rise across both features makes the IF more confident.

group_D_tests.description: group_D: full feature set. OBIS: 1.0.1.29.0.255=energy(Wh), 1.0.2.29.0.255=export_energy(Wh), 1.0.9.29.0.255=apparent_energy(VAh), 1.0.12.27.0.255=voltage(V), 1.0.11.27.0.255=current(A), 1.0.13.27.0.255=PF, 1.0.14.27.0.255=frequency(Hz). Real meters: M1GEMAC1, M2GDDAC1.

normal.description: Normal full-feature reading of M1GEMAC1 after seeded history.

frequency_anomaly.description: Frequency drops to 48.3 Hz on M2GDDAC1 (below 49 Hz safe lower bound). All other parameters remain normal. Indicates grid instability or islanding condition.

batch_request_example.description: Example batch POST /detect with one normal + one energy spike + one voltage sag across three group_A meters. All seeded before '2026-06-18 12:00:00' to use different timestamps.
batch_request_example.seeding_note: If using 12:00 timestamps, re-seed with --before '2026-06-18 12:00:00'. At 12:00 weekday, diurnal load = 0.60 kW -> E = 300 Wh. Adjust or keep --before '2026-06-18 10:00:00' and ensure batch timestamps are after 10:00.

critical_workflow: 1. SEED BASELINE: Run ALL seeding commands at the top of this file. Use --before '2026-06-18 10:00:00'. This plants 48h of 30-min readings ending just before the first test payload timestamp.
2. WAIT: Allow 2-3s for the DB commits to complete before POSTing.
3. SEND TEST: POST records from the relevant test group. Each record is a future reading of a seeded meter.
4. OBSERVE LOGS: Check uvicorn terminal for per-layer trigger reasons and z-score values.

rule_based.note: PF collapse to 0.65 does NOT trigger rule layer (pf_min=0.0 in settings.py). It is a purely ML/statistical anomaly.

layer_triggers.isolation_forest: Complex multivariate anomalies — trained on all 8 anomaly types in generate_dataset.py at correct Wh scale

curl_examples: Energy spike (should fire z-score): Use group_A_tests.zscore_spike payload (SE2303062, E=650.0 Wh)
Voltage sag (should fire rule-based): Use group_A_tests.voltage_sag payload (SE2303062, V=165.0)
Tamper bypass (should fire z-score + IF): Use group_A_tests.tamper_bypass payload (E=105 Wh, I=3.64 A)

expected_behavior.normal_readings: All three layers run. No anomaly flagged. flagged_anomalous=FALSE in DB. Baseline rolls forward.
expected_behavior.zscore_spike: Rule-based: PASS. Z-score: TRIGGERED. IF: likely triggered. flagged_anomalous=TRUE.
expected_behavior.voltage_sag: Rule-based: TRIGGERED. Z-score: PASS (energy normal). IF: likely triggered. flagged_anomalous=TRUE.
expected_behavior.tamper_bypass: Rule-based: PASS. Z-score: TRIGGERED (energy z=-4.7). IF: TRIGGERED (anomalous E/I ratio). flagged_anomalous=TRUE.
expected_behavior.pf_collapse: Rule-based: PASS. Z-score: PASS (energy unchanged). IF: TRIGGERED. flagged_anomalous=TRUE.
expected_behavior.negative_energy: Rule-based: TRIGGERED. Z-score: TRIGGERED. IF: TRIGGERED. All three fire.
expected_behavior.power_factor_invalid: Rule-based: TRIGGERED (PF > 1.0). Z-score: PASS. IF: TRIGGERED.
